title: NVIDIA Vera BlueField-4 STX Brings Agentic AI Storage Processing With In-Silicon Security
author: Nvidia
description: NVIDIA announced new security innovations for Vera BlueField-4 STX, introducing enhanced DOCA Vault, Argus, and Flow capabilities designed to protect agentic AI systems. The platform delivers runtime threat detection up to 1,000x faster than existing solutions while enforcing network and file access policies at speeds up to 800Gb/s. Multiple cybersecurity and storage partners are integrating with the platform, with availability expected in H2 2026.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: NVIDIA is launching advanced security capabilities for its BlueField-4 platform with significant performance improvements (1,000x faster threat detection) and broad ecosystem adoption from major cybersecurity and storage vendors, positioning the company as a leader in secure AI infrastructure.
Akamai is integrating enterprise security solutions with Vera BlueField-4 STX, gaining access to a growing agentic AI market and expanding its security portfolio offerings.
Cisco is among the cybersecurity leaders integrating with the STX platform, positioning itself to serve enterprise AI security needs.
Palo Alto Networks is integrating with Vera BlueField-4 STX, expanding its presence in the emerging agentic AI security market.
CrowdStrike is partnering with NVIDIA on STX-based security solutions, gaining exposure to the growing agentic AI infrastructure market.
Fortinet is integrating security solutions with the BlueField-4 STX platform, positioning itself in the emerging secure AI storage ecosystem.
Dell is building STX-based AI-native storage platforms, enabling it to serve enterprise agentic AI deployments with integrated security.
HPE is developing STX-based storage platforms, positioning itself to capture demand in the secure AI storage infrastructure market.
HPE is developing STX-based storage platforms, positioning itself to capture demand in the secure AI storage infrastructure market.
NetApp is building on STX to deliver AI-native storage infrastructure, expanding its portfolio for enterprise agentic AI deployments.
IBM is among storage providers building STX-based platforms, enabling it to serve the growing agentic AI storage market.
Nutanix is developing STX-based storage platforms, positioning itself in the emerging secure AI infrastructure market.
Accenture is working as a global systems integrator to bring secure AI storage solutions to enterprises, expanding its AI infrastructure consulting services.

title: Akamai Announces Pricing of Upsized Offering of Convertible Senior Notes
author: Na
description: Akamai Technologies announced the pricing of $1.5 billion in 0% convertible senior notes due 2030 and $1.5 billion due 2032, with an option for initial purchasers to buy an additional $500 million. The company plans to use net proceeds of approximately $2.96 billion (or $3.45 billion with full option exercise) to fund Cloud Infrastructure Services capital expenditures and general corporate purposes, including a $350 million share repurchase program.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The company successfully priced a substantial $3 billion convertible notes offering (upsized from $2.6 billion), demonstrating strong investor demand and market confidence. The capital raise supports aggressive expansion of cloud infrastructure services and includes shareholder-friendly share repurchase programs, indicating management confidence in future growth and stock valuation.

title: Stock Market Today: Dow Jones, S&P 500 Futures Decline As Inflation Fears Grip Wall Street— XP, Atmos Energy, GE Aerospace In Focus (UPDATED)
author: Rishabh Mishra
description: U.S. stock futures declined on Tuesday as inflation concerns weighed on markets. The S&P 500 and Nasdaq 100 fell slightly, with the 10-year Treasury yield at 4.60%. Key movers included XP Inc. dropping 6% on weak earnings, Atmos Energy surging 10% after strong Q2 results and dividend increase, and GE Aerospace gaining on a Japan Airlines maintenance deal. Analysts warn the Fed may shift to a tightening bias if inflation pressures persist.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Futures down 0.24% as technology-heavy index faces headwinds from inflation concerns and potential interest rate hikes.
Stock fell 6% in premarket trading due to worse-than-expected Q1 financial results, indicating poor company performance.
Stock jumped 10.13% following strong Q2 earnings with $581.9 million net income and a 14.9% dividend increase to $4.00 per share.
Stock declined 2.50% following announcement of a $1.3 billion senior notes offering, which may dilute shareholder value.
Stock declined slightly 0.13% ahead of earnings report; minimal movement suggests market is awaiting results before forming strong sentiment.

title: Why Are Akamai Shares Trading Higher On Wednesday?
author: Lekha Gupta
description: Akamai Technologies shares rose 4.95% following strong Q1 earnings that beat analyst expectations and a BofA Securities upgrade from Neutral to Buy with a $175 price target. The analyst highlighted the company's transformation into an AI infrastructure platform, driven by a major $1.8 billion seven-year contract and 40% year-over-year growth in Cloud Infrastructure Services. The company projects revenue growth to accelerate from 7% in 2026 to 11% in 2027.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Strong Q1 earnings beat (revenue +0.17% above estimates, EPS +8.78% above estimates), analyst upgrade to Buy with significant price target increase ($130 to $175), major $1.8B contract win, robust growth in AI-driven Cloud Infrastructure Services (40% YoY), and positive forward guidance with accelerating growth projections through 2028.

title: DigitalOcean, Micron, And Rocket Lab Are Among Top 10 Large-Cap Gainers Last Week (May 4-May 8): Are the Others in Your Portfolio?
author: Nabaparna Bhattacharya
description: Wall Street's momentum trade accelerated last week as earnings beats and AI-fueled optimism drove massive rallies across tech and infrastructure stocks. Ten large-cap stocks emerged as top performers, with gains ranging from 32% to 57%, driven by better-than-expected earnings, raised guidance, and major partnerships in cloud computing, semiconductors, and space technology.
